name: ElmAdmin

aliases.name: RouteParams
aliases.type: { pathParams : Dict.Dict String.String String.String, queryParams : Dict.Dict String.String (List.List String.String) }

values.name: admin
values.comment:
Bootstraps your admin application.

    admin
        [ A.page "Home" Home.page
        , A.external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"
        , A.resources "Users"
            { index = Users.index
            , show = Users.show
            , create = Users.create
            , update = Users.update
            }
        , A.group "Workspaces"
            { main = Workspaces.index
            , items =
                [ A.single "Archive" Workspaces.archive
                ]
            }
        ]
        [ preferDarkMode
        , lightTheme ThemeSpec.lightTheme
        ]
        { title = "My Admin"
        , init = init
        }
values.type: List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> List.List (ElmAdmin.Options -> ElmAdmin.Options) -> { title : String.String, init : flags -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ) } -> ElmAdmin.ElmAdmin flags model msg

values.name: darkModeClass
values.comment:
Sets the class added to the DOM when dark mode is on.

Tip: If you're using tailwind's `dark:` variants you might want to set this to `"dark"`.

values.name: darkTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on dark mode.

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Conditionally disable nav links and their pages. They won't be accessible even through urls.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> disable (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (String.String -> ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: dynamic
values.comment:
Dynamic nav link label.

    single "User" User.show
        |> dynamic
            (\{ pathParams } model ->
                Dict.get ":userId" pathParams
                    |> Maybe.andThen (username model)
            )
values.type: (String.String -> ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> String.String) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: external
values.comment:
Used for creating external links in your sidebar.

    external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"

values.name: group
values.comment:
Used for creating grouped pages. Note that the "group" is also a page and if it is hidden or disabled by any means, then the whole group will follow.

    group "Workspace"
        { main = Workspace.index
        , items =
            [ single "New" Workspace.create
            , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
            ]
        }
values.type: String.String -> { main : ElmAdmin.Page model msg, items : List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) } -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: hidden
values.comment:
Conditionally hide nav links.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> hidden (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (String.String -> ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: lightTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on light mode.

values.name: page
values.type: { path : String.String, title : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> String.String, init : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), update : msg -> ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, view : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Html.Html msg } -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg

values.name: params
values.comment:
Manually pass in url params to generate fixed nav links for pages that take in pathParams.

    single "User" User.show
        |> params [ ( ":userId", "123" ) ]
values.type: List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: preferDarkMode
values.comment: Starts the admin on dark mode.

values.name: resourcePage
values.comment: Creates a page with quick access to a "resource".
values.type: { path : String.String, resource : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource, title : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> String.String, init : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), update : msg -> ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, view : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> Html.Html msg } -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg

values.name: resources
values.comment:
A shorthand for creating a group with the pages commonly used for resources.

    resources "Workspaces"
        { index = Workspace.index
        , show = Workspace.show
        , create = Workspace.create
        , update = Workspace.update
        }
values.type: String.String -> { index : ElmAdmin.Page model msg, show : ElmAdmin.Page model msg, create : ElmAdmin.Page model msg, update : ElmAdmin.Page model msg } -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: single
values.comment:
Used for creating a single page with a nav link.

    single "User" User.page

Note that all page links with params will only appear if they are already present on the current page path.

    -- User.page { path = "/users/:userId", ... }

    single "User" User.page

    -- This page link will appear if the user is on "/settings/:userId"
    -- But if the user is on "/", no ":userId" is available so the link will disappear.
values.type: String.String -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: url
values.comment:
Used for creating pages that will not appear as nav links but are still accessible through their direct url.

    url Home.page
values.type: ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: visualGroup
values.comment:
Creates a visual group (The group label itself is not a nav link).

Tip: This can be useful if you want to disable whole sections of your application but you don't want that to be related to one specific root page.

    visualGroup "Workspace"
        [ single "New" Workspace.create
        , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg
